signals.headline: Bitcoin price surges above $78,000 | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Bitcoin price surges above $78,000 | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Bitcoin reached $78,194 at 9:15 a.m. ET on April 22, 2026, up from $76,000 earlier, amid US-Iran ceasefire extension announced late April 21 by President Trump and US Treasury's $15B debt buyback plan injecting liquidity.[2][4]
matched_markets: Will Trump agree to Iranian enrichment of uranium in April?

Current prices confirmed but no new announcements, resolutions, or events today for Ethereum or XRP to drive prediction markets; Bitcoin leads gains.[2]

signals.headline: Iran x Israel/US Conflict Ends by Dec 31 at 100% YES | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Iran x Israel/US Conflict Ends by Dec 31 at 100% YES | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Polymarket's most active X prediction market prices the Iran-Israel/US conflict resolution by December 31, 2026, at 100% YES probability, with $11.9K liquidity and 116 traders, signaling strong crowd consensus on de-escalation despite no confirmed announcements[1].

signals.headline: MicroStrategy discloses record 34,164 BTC purchase on April 20 | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
MicroStrategy discloses record 34,164 BTC purchase on April 20 | YES | HIGH confidence**  
MicroStrategy bought 34,164 BTC (avg. $74,395) from April 13-19 for $2.54B, pushing holdings to 815,061 BTC—largest corporate stash, surpassing BlackRock's ETF; stock surged 25% in 5 days.[1][5]

signals.raw:
Elon Musk Tweet Volume Markets Trending (e.g., Apr 17-24 at 31% for 220-239) | MIXED | MED confidence**  
Active tweet prediction markets like "Elon Musk # tweets April 17-24, 2026?" show 31% on 220-239 range, with others like April total at 28% for 1120-1159; high volumes ($19M+ aggregate) suggest traders watching for mispriced ranges amid no new tweet spikes confirmed[2][3][4].
